title: A Better Way to Build AI
gist: Kreps argues America's AI dominance is bottlenecked not by capital or chips but by local legitimacy—data centers, transmission lines, and power-hungry compute must be sited and permitted in communities whose priorities often clash with national ambitions, unlike China's centrally planned buildout.

insights.claim: The binding constraint on U.S. AI infrastructure buildout is legitimacy at the local level, because data centers, transmission lines, and energy-intensive computing facilities must be sited and permitted in communities whose priorities do not always align with national ambitions.
insights.speaker: Sarah Kreps
insights.so_what: Local permitting and siting friction—not compute or capital—may be the real chokepoint for U.S. AI scaling; watch community-level opposition.
insights.locator: As the United States races to build

insights.claim: Data centers, transmission lines, and energy-intensive computing facilities have become strategic assets central to economic competitiveness and national power.
insights.speaker: Sarah Kreps
insights.so_what: Reframes physical AI infrastructure as national-security assets, implying escalating federal interest in siting outcomes.
insights.locator: As the United States races to build

insights.claim: A widening gap is opening between federal technological strategy and the local governance structures responsible for implementing it, as AI facilities must be sited, permitted, and sustained locally.
insights.speaker: Sarah Kreps
insights.so_what: The federal-local governance mismatch is a structural drag on AI deployment timelines.
insights.locator: The result is a widening gap

insights.claim: The national-versus-local tension is not universal: in China, large-scale digital infrastructure is planned and permitted centrally, avoiding the community-consent friction the U.S. faces.
insights.speaker: Sarah Kreps
insights.so_what: China's centralized permitting is a structural speed advantage in the AI infrastructure race that U.S. governance cannot easily match.
insights.locator: This tension between national ambition and local authority
